tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh --non-interactive from inside $FRAMEWORK exits non-zero (rc=1) with '[FAIL] Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo.' message (PR #18 verified)

framework_bugs.summary: release.yml not generated for mcp_server on full track
framework_bugs.reproduction: bash init.sh --non-interactive --project P --platform mcp_server --track full --deployment organizational --gov-mode private_poc --language python --git-host other --remote-url https://example.com/fake.git --branch-protection-attested --visibility private
framework_bugs.expected: On track=full, .github/workflows/release.yml is generated for every platform (or the omission is documented as expected for mcp_server)
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh prints '[INFO] No release pipeline template for platform mcp_server on host github. Skipping release pipeline.' and skips release.yml; the runbook test 'release_workflow_created' fails. Either ship a release.yml template for mcp_server or add a 'release_workflow_optional_for_platform' carve-out so verify-install / runbook treat it as expected

framework_bugs.summary: platform module mcp_server.md missing from framework
framework_bugs.reproduction: verify-install.sh from a freshly inited mcp_server project reports MANUAL: 'Platform module (mcp_server.md) missing — Copy from orchestrator: docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md'. ls $FRAMEWORK/docs/platform-modules/ shows no mcp_server.md to copy from
framework_bugs.expected: Either ship docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md in the framework, or remove the manual-action prompt for the mcp_server platform
framework_bugs.actual: verify-install emits a manual fix instruction that points to a file that does not exist in the framework source — the user has nowhere to copy it from. Confirms a documentation/template gap

documentation_gaps.gap: verify-install.sh manual action says 'Copy from orchestrator: docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md' but the framework has no such file

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 exits 0; .claude/process-state.json updated with started_at

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: .phase1_architecture.steps_completed is an empty array initially, populates as steps complete

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS completed (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) in sequence with no artifact requirements blocking

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh exits 0 with current_phase still at 0 (consistency-only check, no advancement)

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmation: no script in $FRAMEWORK/scripts/ writes .current_phase=1 after gate passes. grep -rn 'current_phase' shows only readers (validate.sh, check-phase-gate.sh, session-test-gate-check.sh). Test agent had to set it manually with jq to proceed

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after phase 1 gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run start-phase1, complete all 5 steps via --complete-step, run check-phase-gate.sh — verify .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase remains 0
framework_bugs.expected: After phase 1 gate passes, some script (perhaps check-phase-gate.sh --advance, or process-checklist.sh --advance-phase, or an automatic side-effect of completing the last step) writes .current_phase=1 and a gates.phase_0_to_1 timestamp
framework_bugs.actual: current_phase remains 0 indefinitely; no orchestrator-side mechanism exists to advance the phase. Confirms U-D from the 2026-04-25 sweep is still open

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: .build_loop.feature='uat-feat-1' after --start-feature uat-feat-1

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 5 build_loop steps (tests_written, tests_verified_failing, implemented, security_audit, documentation_updated) complete in sequence; security_audit accepted the stub docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeds (pre-commit hook ran gitleaks/Semgrep cleanly)

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` succeeds with rc=0. The local .git/hooks/pre-commit only does gitleaks/Semgrep/TDD-ordering checks; it does NOT call scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh or check BL-006 build-loop state. BL-006 enforcement is only effective via the Claude Code PreToolUse path (verified separately — pre-commit-gate.sh denies when invoked with the right payload)

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 + complete-step build_loop:feature_recorded, started new feature uat-feat-2, completed all build-loop steps, and `git commit -m 'feat(uat-2): ...'` succeeded — confirms PR #17 grace-window reset works (no false-positive BL-006 block on the new feature)

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: With .claude/pending-approval.json present (offered via scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer), feeding a Bash git-commit payload to scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh returns permissionDecision=deny with reason 'commit blocked — pending user decision'. Direct shell `git commit` is NOT blocked by the local pre-commit hook (same surface as U-C)

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removes the sentinel; subsequent pre-commit-gate.sh invocation no longer denies for sentinel reason (it denies for a different reason — phase2_init not verified — but that is correct for this state)

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .features_completed in .claude/build-progress.json contains 'uat-feat-1' (and 'uat-feat-2' after the grace-window test)

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: shell git commit bypasses BL-006
framework_bugs.reproduction: After completing a build loop and recording a feature, run `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): no loop'` directly in the project. Commit succeeds with rc=0
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should block any feat: commit that lacks an associated --start-feature build-loop. The runbook acknowledges this is U-C and still open
framework_bugs.actual: The .git/hooks/pre-commit installed by init.sh contains only gitleaks + Semgrep + test-co-location checks. It does not invoke scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh or check the build_loop state. Enforcement only fires via the Claude Code PreToolUse hook surface, which a direct shell session does not exercise. Confirms U-C from the 2026-04-25 sweep is still open

documentation_gaps.gap: BL-006 / BL-010 expectation that .git/hooks/pre-commit chains scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh is unimplemented; runbook calls this U-C

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase3 exits 0 (warns about 2 features since last UAT but still starts the validation block)

tests.name: start_uat_creates_session
tests.details: .uat_session.session_id=1 with started_at set after --start-uat 1

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: lint-uat-scenarios.sh prints usage when called with no argument and exits 0; no populated UAT scenarios HTML exists in this stub project (no real linting performed). Effectively N/A for the bare-init state

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched..gate_passed) completed in sequence via --complete-step uat_session:STEP

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh exits non-zero (15 inconsistencies found — manual phase advancement plus BL-006 bypass commits trigger the consistency check correctly). For a clean run with proper attestations the gate would pass; the failure here is the expected behavior of the consistency check, not a bug

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero

tests.details: N/A for private_poc

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: git ls-files | xargs grep for 'password=|api_key=|secret=|token=' assignment patterns finds no real secrets (broad keyword search hits docs/templates that mention these as concepts only)

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: .claude/process-audit.log does not exist — no FORCE/reset events occurred (N/A)

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find .claude -name '*.tmp' returns nothing

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only ?? .claude/ and ?? Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ (untracked, expected). git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches captured SHA). Framework-self guard from PR #18 held

summary: Base private_poc/mcp_server/full sweep: init --non-interactive succeeds, framework-self guard fires, BL-015 sentinel blocks via PreToolUse path, BL-006 grace window resets correctly. Two known-open prior-sweep bugs (U-C bl006 git-hook gap, U-D no current_phase advancement) re-confirmed as Medium. Two new-Medium gaps surfaced for mcp_server platform: missing release.yml template and missing docs/platform-modules/mcp_server.md (verify-install instructs user to copy a file the framework does not ship).
